extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is a long, wide low plateau (valley) in the middle of the series, where values drop clearly below the baseline level and stay low for many time steps.
R2: On both sides of this valley the signal is around a medium baseline (about 0.45–0.55) for a long duration.
R3: At one end of the series there is a single, short positive spike that rises clearly above the surrounding baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts near a medium baseline level (around 0.45–0.5).
R2: After an initial baseline segment, there is one long, wide high plateau where the values rise well above baseline and stay high for many time steps.
R3: After this high plateau, the signal drops back down close to the initial baseline and stays there to the end; there are no deep valleys or sharp spikes.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is a long, wide low plateau (valley) in the middle of the series, where values drop clearly below the baseline level and stay low for many time steps.
R2: On at least one side of this valley, the signal returns to a clear baseline plateau (similar level to the start of the series).
R3: In addition to the main valley, there is at least one isolated, narrow deep dip (a sharp downward spike) that is much lower than the surrounding values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series contains one long plateau near the middle whose values are clearly lower than the values at the beginning and at the end.  
R2: Before and after this low plateau, the series stays at a medium level (around the same height at start and end).  
R3: There is at most one tall, narrow peak occurring near one end of the series (either early or late).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long plateau in the middle whose values are clearly higher than the values at the beginning and at the end.  
R2: The values at the beginning and at the end are both at a similar, lower level.  
R3: There are no deep dips or additional tall peaks besides the single central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long plateau whose values are clearly lower than the surrounding segments.  
R2: On at least one side of this low plateau there is a deep, narrow dip that goes even lower than the plateau itself.  
R3: The parts of the series outside the low plateau and dips stay at a roughly medium level, similar at the beginning and at the end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal spends a long middle segment at a low level (around 0.1–0.2) clearly below its initial and final levels.  
R2: Before the low segment, the signal is at a medium level (around 0.45–0.5) for a noticeable duration.  
R3: After the low segment, the signal rises back toward the initial medium level; any high peak is short and occurs near one edge (start or end), not forming a long plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts at a medium baseline level (around 0.47–0.5).  
R2: In the middle, there is a single long high plateau (around 0.65–0.85) that is clearly above the baseline.  
R3: After this plateau, the signal drops back to approximately the same medium baseline level as at the beginning, with no additional deep dips or secondary high peaks.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal contains a clear long low plateau (around 0.1–0.2) in the middle, lower than both the beginning and the end.  
R2: In addition to the long low plateau, there is at least one extra sharp, deep dip (short in time) either before or after the plateau.  
R3: Outside the low regions, the signal returns to a higher baseline (around 0.45–0.5), similar at the beginning and end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long “low plateau” segment in the middle (roughly between indices 40–100) that is clearly lower than both the early and late parts of the series.  
R2: The long low plateau is preceded or followed by a short, pronounced spike (sharp local maximum) that rises well above the surrounding level.  
R3: Outside the low plateau, the signal remains relatively flat and moderate (no long high plateau like in Class 1).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a long “high plateau” segment (roughly between indices 40–110) that is clearly higher than the early and late parts of the series.  
R2: The portions before and after the high plateau stay at a relatively similar, lower and flat baseline level.  
R3: There are no additional strong spikes or deep dips outside the high plateau (the shape is basically “flat–high–flat”).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains a long plateau (either high or low) plus at least one very deep, narrow dip (sharp local minimum) that cuts far below the surrounding level.  
R2: At least one of the strong dips occurs near one of the edges of the plateau (early, late, or both), creating a “notched” plateau.  
R3: The baseline outside the plateau(s) is relatively flat, similar to Class 0 and 1, but the distinguishing feature is the presence of these very sharp, isolated deep dips.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long low plateau (duration ≥ ~40 samples) in the middle, clearly below the surrounding baseline level.  
R2: Before and after this low plateau, the signal stays close to a mid‑level baseline (around 0.45–0.55).  
R3: There is at most one short spike above the baseline and, if present, it is located near one end of the series (beginning or end), not in the central low region.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau (duration ≥ ~40 samples) in the middle, clearly above the surrounding baseline level.  
R2: Both before and after this high plateau, the signal stays close to a lower, roughly constant baseline.  
R3: No deep dips exist inside the high plateau; the plateau top is relatively flat.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains a long plateau (either high or low) like classes 0 and 1, but additionally shows a very sharp, deep dip (narrow valley) that is much lower than the nearby baseline.  
R2: This deep dip is located near one end of the series (early or late), outside the main central plateau.  
R3: There is exactly one such pronounced dip; outside it, the behavior resembles either a class‑0‑like or class‑1‑like pattern.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has two flat, moderate plateaus (around the same level) with one long, low plateau in between.  
R2: The low plateau is much longer in duration than any high peak in the series.  
R3: Any tall peak (significantly higher than the plateaus) is short and occurs near one end of the series.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau centered in time, with similar low plateaus before and after it.  
R2: The high plateau is substantially higher than the preceding and following regions but roughly constant in value.  
R3: There are no deep, narrow dips that cut below the low plateaus.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains at least one deep, narrow dip that is much lower than its neighboring values.  
R2: These deep dips occur while the surrounding regions are relatively flat (either on a high or moderate plateau).  
R3: The overall shape may include a long low or high plateau, but the defining feature is the presence of one or more sharp, deep valleys.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has two long plateaus at a medium level (~0.45–0.55), one at the beginning and one at the end.  
R2: Between those plateaus there is one long segment at a clearly lower level (~0.1–0.2).  
R3: There is at most one short, clearly higher spike (~0.7–0.85) attached to either the beginning or the end of one of the medium plateaus.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts with a medium, roughly constant baseline (~0.45–0.5).  
R2: After this, there is one long plateau at a distinctly higher level (~0.65–0.85).  
R3: The series then returns to approximately the original medium baseline level and stays there to the end.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series contains one long low segment (~0.1–0.2) in the middle, clearly below the surrounding values.  
R2: Both before and after this low segment, the series returns to a higher baseline (~0.45–0.55).  
R3: In addition, there is at least one short, sharp dip or notch inside one of the high-baseline regions, going significantly below that baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long middle segment where the value is clearly lower than at the beginning and end (a wide “valley” between two higher plateaus).  
R2: One end of the series (either early or late) contains a relatively sharp, isolated peak that rises above the surrounding plateau.  
R3: Outside the valley and the possible single peak, the signal stays roughly flat (low variability) at a medium level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a long middle segment where the value is clearly higher than at the beginning and end (a wide “hill” between two lower plateaus).  
R2: The transitions into and out of this high middle segment are steep, but there are no extra sharp peaks or dips inside or outside the hill.  
R3: The beginning and ending segments are roughly flat at a similar, lower level than the middle segment.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long segment (either in the middle or near one side) with values clearly lower than the surrounding parts (a deep, wide valley).  
R2: In addition to the wide valley, there is at least one extra narrow, sharp dip (a spike downward) that is much lower than the nearby baseline.  
R3: Outside the valleys and sharp dips, the signal is roughly flat at a medium level.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has one long low plateau roughly in the middle (values clearly below the surrounding baseline).
R2: Before and after this low plateau, the signal stays near a mid‑level baseline (around 0.45–0.55) for a noticeable duration.
R3: There is a single distinct high spike or short high plateau near one edge of the series, higher than both the baseline and the low plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau in the middle (values clearly above the surrounding baseline).
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal stays near a lower, relatively flat baseline.
R3: There are no extra deep dips or high spikes outside this central high plateau; the shape is essentially “low–high–low” only.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long plateau (either clearly higher or clearly lower than the baseline) occupying the middle portion.
R2: On one or both sides of this long plateau, there is at least one very sharp, narrow dip to a value much lower than the adjacent baseline.
R3: The overall shape is characterized by these pronounced narrow dips in addition to the single long plateau (unlike Class 0 and Class 1, which have at most one such extreme excursion).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long low plateau (values clearly below the baseline, around 0.1–0.2) in the middle of the time axis.
R2: Before and after this low plateau, the signal returns to a mid-level baseline (around 0.45–0.5) for a noticeable duration.
R3: There is at most one short high spike (above about 0.7) and, if present, it is located near one side of the long low plateau (not centered).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has one long high plateau (values clearly above the baseline, around 0.7–0.8) in the middle of the time axis.
R2: Before and after this high plateau, the signal stays near a lower, roughly constant baseline (around 0.47–0.5).
R3: There are no additional deep drops or extra spikes of comparable magnitude outside this single central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long plateau (either high or low) occupying the middle of the time axis, similar in length to the plateaus of the other classes.
R2: In addition to this long plateau, there is at least one very sharp, narrow dip to a much lower value (around 0.1–0.2) occurring near one end of the series.
R3: These sharp dips are brief compared with the main plateau and clearly stand out from the surrounding baseline or plateau level.
